author: Admilson B. F. Cossa
authority: runtime source and npm run verify

claims.title: run.race cancels losing branches
claims.limitations: Provider billing or remote execution stops only when the underlying provider honors AbortSignal.

claims.title: run.retry stops on parent cancellation
claims.expectedInvariant: no extra retry attempt runs after cancellation is observed
claims.limitations: The guarantee applies to WorkIt retry sleeps and task bodies that observe ctx.signal.

claims.title: run.bracket cleanup timeout is bounded and observable
claims.expectedInvariant: a hanging cleanup emits task:cleanup_timeout and the owner settles
claims.limitations: The timeout bounds WorkIt cleanup waiting; external systems still need their own timeout and idempotency policies.

claims.title: budget inputs are immutable boundary values
claims.expectedInvariant: caller input object is not mutated; final spent value is read from run.context.budget
claims.limitations: Applications must read runtime budget state through the context API rather than expecting mutation of the original object.

claims.title: channel capacity applies producer backpressure
claims.expectedInvariant: third send to capacity-two channel blocks until a receive drains one item
claims.limitations: This proof covers local channel capacity, not distributed queue semantics.

claims.title: diagnostics report stable cleanup finding codes
claims.expectedInvariant: cleanup timeout events produce cleanup_timeout findings
claims.limitations: Rich rendering and exporter behavior are separate optional surfaces.

claims.title: retry policy rejects unsafe attempt counts
claims.expectedInvariant: unbounded retry counts are rejected at the policy boundary
claims.limitations: The cap prevents accidental huge retry policies; application-level retry budgets may be stricter.

claims.title: worker offload rejects remote and executable URL schemes
claims.expectedInvariant: data, http, https, and javascript worker inputs are rejected before import
claims.limitations: Local file URLs remain application authority; WorkIt does not sandbox arbitrary trusted local code.

claims.title: worker timeout terminates non-cooperative CPU work
claims.expectedInvariant: late marker is not written after offload timeout
claims.limitations: The hard boundary applies to worker offload; JavaScript cannot preempt a CPU loop on the main thread.

claims.title: public proof artifact exposes release evidence
claims.expectedInvariant: benchmarks/public-proof.json exposes commands, fixtures, guides, and runtime matrix
claims.limitations: This verifies repository artifact shape, not an external registry publication event.

claims.title: release policy documents signed tags and worker boundary
claims.expectedInvariant: SECURITY.md documents signed tags and structured-clone worker input
claims.limitations: Documentation must remain aligned with the actual release workflow.

claims.title: release provenance gate verifies tag policy
claims.expectedInvariant: release-provenance script contains signed-tag verification logic
claims.limitations: A dry-run gate cannot guarantee a future maintainer will sign a tag; it verifies that the repository policy gate exists.

claims.title: article benchmark suite has expected executable coverage
claims.expectedInvariant: benchmarks/articles contains exactly 19 numbered benchmark scripts
claims.limitations: Coverage count does not prove a benchmark result; individual benchmark assertions do.

claims.title: captured article benchmark result is machine-readable
claims.limitations: Timing values are representative for the captured machine and run; semantic assertions are the portable claim.
